title: Latent neutralization/weather shock expert
summary: F1 strategy changes sharply when Safety Car/VSC windows open or grip suddenly drops; official analysis notes reduced pit-loss under Safety Car and teams can pre-arm stops for those windows.
rationale: F1 strategy changes sharply when Safety Car/VSC windows open or grip suddenly drops; official analysis notes reduced pit-loss under Safety Car and teams can pre-arm stops for those windows. The dataset has no explicit flag-state column, so hidden neutralization/wet-shift laps may be recoverable from same-lap field-wide spikes in LapTime, LapTime_Delta, multi-car Position_Change, and clustered PitStop activity.
implementation_hint: Create per-(Year,Race,LapNumber) shock features: field median/iqr of LapTime_Delta, fraction of cars with extreme slow laps, fraction pitting, spread of Position_Change, and deviation from prior 3-lap baselines. Train either a small regime classifier or a gated expert that only activates on high-shock laps, then blend with the base tabular model via OOF stacking.
expected_effect: Should improve recall on abrupt strategy laps that tyre-age formulas miss, especially free-stop or sudden wet-transition behavior.
risk: Field aggregates can become noisy if inferred from shuffled rows or if test races have different shock prevalence; must compute strictly within current lap only.
